relations.evidence: In Odoo *Maintenance*
users can access the *Maintenance Calendar*

relations.target: create, schedule, and edit both corrective and preventive maintenance requests
relations.evidence: to create, schedule, and edit both corrective and preventive maintenance requests
corrective and preventive maintenance
corrective and preventive maintenance
navigate to Maintenance app --> Maintenance --> Maintenance Calendar.
Maintenance requests can be created directly from the *Maintenance Calendar*.
To create a new request, click anywhere on the calendar.
Doing so opens a New Event pop-up window.
In the Name: field, assign a title to the new request.
Clicking Create on the pop-up window saves the new request.
If the request's creation should be cancelled, click Cancel.
To add more details..., click Edit.
Clicking Edit opens a blank maintenance request form.
Product: Odoo. To access..., navigate to Maintenance app.
In the Request field, assign a title to the new request.
In the Created By field, from the drop-down menu, select which user the request was created by.
In the For field, from the drop-down menu, select if this request is being created for a piece of Equipment, or a Work Center.
select if this request is being created for a piece of Equipment, or a Work Center.
select if this request is being created for a piece of Equipment, or a Work Center.
If Work Center is selected in the For field's drop-down menu, two additional fields appear on the form: Work Center and Block Workcenter.
If Work Center is selected in the For field's drop-down menu, two additional fields appear on the form: Work Center and Block Workcenter.
If Equipment is selected in the For field, which it is by default, select which machine or tool requires maintenance from the Equipment field.
Once a specific piece of equipment is selected, a greyed-out Category field appears
listing the Equipment Category to which the equipment belongs.
In the Worksheet Template field, if necessary, click the drop-down menu to select a worksheet template.
These templates are custom templates
Under the Category field, the Request Date field displays the date requested for the maintenance to happen.
The Maintenance Type field provides two selectable radio button options: Corrective and Preventive.
Corrective and Preventive.
Corrective and Preventive.
If this request is tied to a specific , select that from the Manufacturing Order field.
From the drop-down menu for the Team field, select the desired maintenance team who will perform the maintenance.
In the Responsible field, select the technician responsible for the request.
In the Scheduled Date field, click the date to open a calendar popover.
In the Duration field, enter the the amount of hours (in a 00:00 format) that the maintenance is planned to take.
In the Priority field, choose a priority between one and three ⭐⭐⭐ (stars).
If working in a multi-company environment, from the drop-down menu in the Company field, select the company to which this maintenance request belongs.
In the Notes tab, type out any internal notes
In the Instructions tab, if necessary, select one of the three radio button options
The available methods for providing instructions are via PDF, Google Slide, or Text.
The available methods for providing instructions are via PDF, Google Slide, or Text.
The available methods for providing instructions are via PDF, Google Slide, or Text.
Product: Odoo, The *Maintenance Calendar*
The *Maintenance Calendar* provides various views
The *Maintenance Calendar* provides search functions
The *Maintenance Calendar* provides filters
to help keep track of the progress of ongoing and planned maintenance requests
to help keep track of the progress of ongoing and planned maintenance requests
To access the maintenance calendar, navigate to Maintenance app --> Maintenance --> Maintenance Calendar.
The left-hand side of the resulting drop-down menu lists all the different Filters users can select. By default, To Do and Active are selected.
The left-hand side of the resulting drop-down menu lists all the different Filters users can select. By default, To Do and Active are selected.
To add a custom filter to the Maintenance Calendar, click Add Custom Filter, under the Filters section of the drop-down menu.
This opens an Add Custom Filter pop-up window.
From this pop-up window, configure the properties of the new rule for the filter.
Once ready, click Add.
the Favorites, or any searches that have been saved as a favorite
To save a new Favorite search, select the desired Filters. Then, click Save current search.
In the field directly below Save current search, assign a name to the search.
Under the assigned name, there are two options, to save the current search either as the Default filter, or as a Shared filter.
Under the assigned name, there are two options, to save the current search either as the Default filter, or as a Shared filter.
Selecting Default filter sets this filter as the default when opening this calendar view.
Selecting the Shared filter makes this filter available to other users.
Once ready, click Save.
The Maintenance Calendar is available in six different views: Calendar (default)
The Maintenance Calendar is available in six different views: Kanban
The Maintenance Calendar is available in six different views: List
The Maintenance Calendar is available in six different views: Pivot
The Maintenance Calendar is available in six different views: Graph
The Maintenance Calendar is available in six different views: Activity
Product: Odoo
Calendar is the default view displayed when the Maintenance Calendar is opened.
At the far-right side of the page is a sidebar column...
sidebar column, containing a minimized calendar...
sidebar column, containing... a Technician list
Technician list, displaying all the Technicians...
Technician list... displaying all the Technicians with requests currently open.
individual technicians are only listed, if they are listed as Responsible on at least one maintenance request form.
With the Kanban view, all open maintenance requests are displayed
Each maintenance request appears on its own task card
displayed in Kanban-style columns, in their respective stages of the maintenance process
in their respective stages of the maintenance process
displayed in Kanban-style columns
Hovering at the top of a column reveals a ⚙️ (gear) icon
Clicking the ⚙️ (gear) icon reveals a list of options for that column: Fold
Clicking the ⚙️ (gear) icon reveals a list of options for that column: Edit
Clicking the ⚙️ (gear) icon reveals a list of options for that column: Automations
Clicking the ⚙️ (gear) icon reveals a list of options for that column: Delete
Clicking Edit opens an Edit: (stage name) pop-up window
The following are the column options that can be edited: Name
The following are the column options that can be edited: Folded in Maintenance Pipe
The following are the column options that can be edited: Request Confirmed
The following are the column options that can be edited: Sequence
The following are the column options that can be edited: Request Done
Folded in Maintenance Pipe: when checked, this stage's column is folded by default in the Kanban view type
and the maintenance request type is set to *Work Center*
If the box *is* ticked, the work center is automatically blocked
if ticked, this box indicates this stage is the final step of the maintenance process
Once ready, click Save & Close
If no changes have been made, click Discard
click the X icon to close the pop-up window
Product: Odoo, Title: List view
all open maintenance requests are displayed in a list
Subjects: the name assigned to the maintenance request.
Employee: the employee who originally created the maintenance request.
Technician: the technician responsible for the maintenance request.
Category: the category the equipment being repaired belongs to.
Stage: the stage of the maintenance process the request is currently in.
Company: if in a multi-company environment, the company in the database the request is assigned to.

Product: Odoo
Title: Pivot view
Pivot view selected, maintenance requests are displayed in a pivot table
maintenance requests are displayed in a pivot table
To add more data to the pivot table, click the Measures button
By default, Count is selected
Additional options to add to the table are Additional Leaves to Plan Ahead
Additional options... Duration
Additional options... Repeat Every
To the right of the Measures button is the Insert in Spreadsheet button
